105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )

156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}

223	        if fmt_type == "tag":

225	                structural_format.get("content")

227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233

236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):

261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:

324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

569	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
577	                )
578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,
586	                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return
590	
591	                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
592	                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
593	            if isinstance(comp, list):
594	                self.text_ += comp[0]
595	            else:
596	                assert isinstance(comp, str)
597	                self.text_ += comp
598	
599	            self.variables[name] = comp
600	            self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
601	            self.variable_event[name].set()
602	        else:
603	            assert (
604	                self.num_api_spec_tokens is None
605	            ), "stream is not supported with api speculative execution"
606	            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(
607	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
608	            )
609	
610	            self.variables[name] = ""
611	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
612	
613	            for comp, meta_info in generator:
614	                self.text_ += comp
615	                self.variables[name] += comp
616	                self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
617	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
618	                self.stream_text_event.set()
619	
620	            self.variable_event[name].set()
621	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
622	
623	    def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
624	        choices_decision = self.backend.select(
625	            self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
626	        )
627	        if expr.name is not None:
628	            name = expr.name
629	            self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision
630	            self.meta_info[name] = choices_decision.meta_info
631	            self.variable_event[name].set()
632	            if self.stream_var_event:
633	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
634	        self.text_ += choices_decision.decision
635	
636	    def _execute_variable(self, expr: SglVariable):
637	        src_executor = expr.source_stream_executor
638	        value = src_executor.get_var(expr.name)
639	        self._execute_fill(value)
640	
641	    def _execute_role_begin(self, expr: SglRoleBegin):
642	        assert self.cur_role is None, "Nested roles are not allowed."
643	
644	        if len(self.messages_) == 0 and expr.role != "system":
645	            # Insert the default system message
646	            default_system = self.chat_template.default_system_prompt
647	            if default_system:
648	                self._execute_role_begin(SglRoleBegin("system"))
649	                self._execute_fill(default_system)
650	                self._execute_role_end(SglRoleEnd("system"))
651	
652	        self.cur_role = expr.role
653	
654	        prefix, _ = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
655	
656	        self._execute_fill(prefix, prefix=True)
657	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = len(self.text_)
658	
659	    def _execute_role_end(self, expr: SglRoleEnd):
660	        if (
661	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
662	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
663	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
664	        ):
665	            # Execute the stored lazy generation calls
666	            self.backend.role_end_generate(self)
667	        self.cur_role = None
668	
669	        new_text = self.text_[self.cur_role_begin_pos :].lstrip()
670	
671	        _, suffix = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
672	        self._execute_fill(suffix)
673	
674	        if self.cur_images:
675	            # OpenAI vision API format
676	            last_msg = {
677	                "role": expr.role,
678	                "content": [{"type": "text", "text": new_text}],
679	            }
680	            for image_path, image_base64_data in self.cur_images:
681	                last_msg["content"].append(
682	                    {
683	                        "type": "image_url",
684	                        "image_url": {
685	                            "url": f"data:image/jpeg;base64,{image_base64_data}"
686	                        },
687	                    }
688	                )

142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one

350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix
21	
22	
23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50	
51	
52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs
88	
89	
90	def _get_tool_schema(tool: Tool) -> dict:
91	    return {
92	        "properties": {
93	            "name": {"type": "string", "enum": [tool.function.name]},
94	            "parameters": (
95	                tool.function.parameters
96	                if tool.function.parameters
97	                else {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
98	            ),
99	        },
100	        "required": ["name", "parameters"],
101	    }
102	
103	
104	def infer_type_from_json_schema(schema: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
105	    """
106	    Infer the primary type of a parameter from JSON Schema.
107	
108	    Supports complex JSON Schema structures including:
109	    - Direct type field (including type arrays)
110	    - anyOf/oneOf: parameter can be any of multiple types
111	    - enum: parameter must be one of enum values
112	    - allOf: parameter must satisfy all type definitions
113	    - properties: inferred as object type
114	    - items: inferred as array type
115	
116	    Args:
117	        schema: JSON Schema definition
118	
119	    Returns:
120	        Inferred type ('string', 'number', 'object', 'array', etc.) or None
121	    """
122	    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
123	        return None
124	
125	    # Priority 1: Direct type field (including type arrays)
126	    if "type" in schema:
127	        type_value = schema["type"]
128	        if isinstance(type_value, str):
129	            return type_value
130	        elif isinstance(type_value, list) and type_value:
131	            # Handle type arrays: return first non-null type
132	            non_null_types = [t for t in type_value if t != "null"]
133	            if non_null_types:
134	                return non_null_types[0]
135	            return "string"  # If only null, default to string
136	
137	    # Priority 2: Handle anyOf/oneOf
138	    if "anyOf" in schema or "oneOf" in schema:
139	        schemas = schema.get("anyOf") or schema.get("oneOf")
140	        types = []
141	
142	        if isinstance(schemas, list):
143	            for sub_schema in schemas:
144	                inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
145	                if inferred_type:
146	                    types.append(inferred_type)
147	
148	            if types:
149	                # If all types are the same, return unified type
150	                if len(set(types)) == 1:
151	                    return types[0]
152	                # When types differ, prioritize string (safest)
153	                if "string" in types:
154	                    return "string"
155	                # Otherwise return first type
156	                return types[0]
157	
158	    # Priority 3: Handle enum (infer type from enum values)
159	    if "enum" in schema and isinstance(schema["enum"], list):
160	        if not schema["enum"]:
161	            return "string"
162	
163	        # Infer type from enum values
164	        enum_types = set()
165	        for value in schema["enum"]:
166	            if value is None:
167	                enum_types.add("null")
168	            elif isinstance(value, bool):
169	                enum_types.add("boolean")
170	            elif isinstance(value, int):
171	                enum_types.add("integer")
172	            elif isinstance(value, float):
173	                enum_types.add("number")
174	            elif isinstance(value, str):
175	                enum_types.add("string")
176	            elif isinstance(value, list):
177	                enum_types.add("array")
178	            elif isinstance(value, dict):
179	                enum_types.add("object")
180	
181	        # If type is uniform, return that type
182	        if len(enum_types) == 1:
183	            return enum_types.pop()
184	        # Mixed types, prioritize string
185	        return "string"
186	
187	    # Priority 4: Handle allOf (must satisfy all types)
188	    if "allOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["allOf"], list):
189	        schemas = schema["allOf"]
190	        for sub_schema in schemas:
191	            inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
192	            if inferred_type and inferred_type != "string":
193	                return inferred_type
194	        return "string"
195	
196	    # Priority 5: Infer object type
197	    if "properties" in schema:
198	        return "object"
199	
200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type

4	def is_legacy_structural_tag(obj: Dict) -> bool:
5	    # test whether an object is a legacy structural tag
6	    # see `StructuralTagResponseFormat` at `sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.protocol`
7	    if obj.get("structures", None) is not None:
8	        assert obj.get("triggers", None) is not None
9	        return True
10	    else:
11	        assert obj.get("format", None) is not None
12	        return False
13

200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type
201	    if "items" in schema:
202	        return "array"
203	
204	    return None

206	
207	def get_json_schema_constraint(
208	    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
209	) -> Optional[dict]:
210	    """
211	    Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        tool_choice: The tool choice specification
215	
216	    Returns:
217	        JSON schema dict, or None if no valid tools found
218	    """
219	
220	    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
221	        # For specific function choice, return the user's parameters schema directly
222	        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
223	        for tool in tools:
224	            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
225	                return {
226	                    "type": "array",
227	                    "minItems": 1,
228	                    "maxItems": 1,
229	                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
230	                }
231	        return None
232	    elif tool_choice == "required":
233	        json_schema = {
234	            "type": "array",
235	            "minItems": 1,
236	            "items": {
237	                "type": "object",
238	                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
239	            },
240	        }
241	        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
242	        if json_schema_defs:
243	            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
244	        return json_schema
245	
246	    return None

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex

828	class ProgramState:
829	    """The state of an SGL program."""
830	
831	    def __init__(self, stream_executor: StreamExecutor):
832	        self.stream_executor = stream_executor
833	
834	    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
835	        if expr is not None:
836	            role_expr = SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
837	            self.stream_executor.submit(role_expr)
838	            return role_expr
839	        else:
840	
841	            @contextmanager
842	            def role_scope():
843	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleBegin(name))
844	                yield
845	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleEnd(name))
846	
847	            return role_scope()
848	
849	    def system(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
850	        return self._role_common("system", expr)
851	
852	    def user(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
853	        return self._role_common("user", expr)
854	
855	    def assistant(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
856	        return self._role_common("assistant", expr)
857	
858	    @contextmanager
859	    def var_scope(self, name: str):
860	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeBegin(name))
861	        yield
862	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeEnd(name))
863	
864	    def fork(
865	        self,
866	        size: int = 1,
867	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
868	    ):
869	        stream_executors = self.stream_executor.fork(size, position_ids_offset)
870	        states = [ProgramState(x) for x in stream_executors]
871	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
872	        return state_group
873	
874	    @contextmanager
875	    def copy(self, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
876	        state_group = self.fork(1, position_ids_offset)
877	        try:
878	            yield state_group[0]
879	        finally:
880	            state_group.join()
881	
882	    def text(self):
883	        return self.stream_executor.text()
884	
885	    def messages(self):
886	        return self.stream_executor.messages()
887	
888	    def sync(self):
889	        return self.stream_executor.sync()
890	
891	    def error(self):
892	        return self.stream_executor.error()
893	
894	    def text_iter(self, var_name: Optional[str] = None):
895	        if self.stream_executor.stream:
896	            prev = 0
897	            if var_name is None:
898	                event = self.stream_executor.stream_text_event
899	                while True:
900	                    event.wait()
901	                    event.clear()
902	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.text_[prev:])
903	                    prev += len(out)
904	                    if out:
905	                        yield out
906	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
907	                        break
908	            else:
909	                event = None
910	                while not event:
911	                    if var_name in self.stream_executor.stream_var_event:
912	                        event = self.stream_executor.stream_var_event[var_name]
913	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
914	                        yield ""
915	                        return
916	
917	                while True:
918	                    event.wait()
919	                    event.clear()
920	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.variables[var_name][prev:])
921	                    prev += len(out)
922	                    if out:
923	                        yield out
924	                    if self.stream_executor.variable_event[var_name].is_set():
925	                        break
926	        else:
927	            if var_name is None:
928	                yield self.text()
929	            else:
930	                yield self.get_var(var_name)
931

3	
4	from typing import List, Optional, Union
5	
6	import torch
7	import triton
8	import triton.language as tl
9	
10	from sglang.srt.utils import get_device_core_count
11	
12	
13	@triton.jit
14	def apply_token_bitmask_inplace_kernel(
15	    logits_ptr,
16	    bitmask_ptr,
17	    indices_ptr,
18	    num_rows,
19	    vocab_size,
20	    logits_strides,
21	    bitmask_strides,
22	    NUM_SMS: tl.constexpr,
23	    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
24	):
25	    """Apply a bitmask to logits in-place using Triton. The bitmask is a 01 bitwise compressed tensor,
26	    where 0 means the token is masked and 1 means the token is not masked. After applying the bitmask,
27	    the masked logits will be set to -inf.
28	
29	    Parameters
30	    ----------
31	    logits_ptr : tl.tensor
32	        Pointer to the logits tensor to apply the bitmask to.
33	
34	    bitmask_ptr : tl.tensor
35	        Pointer to the bitmask tensor to apply.
36	
37	    indices_ptr : Optional[tl.tensor]
38	        Optional pointer to indices tensor specifying which rows to apply the mask to.
39	
40	    num_rows : int
41	        Number of rows to process. If indices_ptr is provided, this is the number of unique indices.
42	
43	    vocab_size : int
44	        Size of the vocabulary dimension. If the logits does not have a vocab padding, this is the
45	        same as the logits's second dimension. Otherwise, this is the actual size of the vocabulary.
46	
47	    logits_strides : int
48	        Stride between rows in the logits tensor.
49	
50	    bitmask_strides : int
51	        Stride between rows in the bitmask tensor.
52	
53	    NUM_SMS : int
54	        Number of streaming multiprocessors to use.
55	
56	    BLOCK_SIZE : int
57	        Size of processing blocks.
58	    """
59	
60	    pid = tl.program_id(0)
61	    num_blocks = tl.cdiv(vocab_size, BLOCK_SIZE)
62	    for work_id in tl.range(pid, num_rows * num_blocks, NUM_SMS):
63	        row_id = work_id // num_blocks
64	        block_offset = (work_id % num_blocks) * BLOCK_SIZE
65	        batch_id = row_id if indices_ptr is None else tl.load(indices_ptr + row_id)
66	        offsets = block_offset + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)
67	        bitmask_offsets = block_offset // 32 + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE // 32)
68	        vocab_mask = offsets < vocab_size
69	        packed_bitmask_mask = bitmask_offsets < bitmask_strides
70	        packed_bitmask = tl.load(
71	            bitmask_ptr + batch_id * bitmask_strides + bitmask_offsets,
72	            packed_bitmask_mask,
73	        )
74	        bitmask = ((packed_bitmask[:, None] >> (tl.arange(0, 32)[None, :])) & 1) == 0
75	        bitmask = bitmask.reshape(BLOCK_SIZE)
76	
77	        tl.store(
78	            logits_ptr + batch_id * logits_strides + offsets,
79	            -float("inf"),
80	            vocab_mask & bitmask,
81	        )
82	
83	
84	def apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(
85	    logits: torch.Tensor,
86	    bitmask: torch.Tensor,
87	    indices: Optional[Union[List[int], torch.Tensor]] = None,
88	):
89	    NUM_SMS = get_device_core_count()
90	    BLOCK_SIZE = 4096
91	    BITS_PER_BLOCK = 32
92	
93	    # Check input dtype
94	    assert bitmask.dtype == torch.int32, "bitmask must be of type int32"
95	
96	    # Check input tensor shapes.
97	    logits_shape = logits.shape
98	    bitmask_shape = bitmask.shape
99	    if logits.ndim == 1:
100	        logits_shape = (1, logits_shape[0])
101	    if bitmask.ndim == 1:
102	        bitmask_shape = (1, bitmask_shape[0])
103	
104	    required_bitmask_width = (logits_shape[1] + BITS_PER_BLOCK - 1) // BITS_PER_BLOCK
105	    assert required_bitmask_width >= bitmask_shape[1], (
106	        f"Bitmask width too large: allow at most {required_bitmask_width} int32s for "
107	        f"logits' width {logits_shape[1]}, but got {bitmask_shape[1]}"
108	    )
109	
110	    vocab_size = min(logits_shape[1], bitmask_shape[1] * BITS_PER_BLOCK)
111	
112	    num_rows = None
113	    if isinstance(indices, list) or isinstance(indices, torch.Tensor):
114	        indices = torch.tensor(indices, dtype=torch.int32, device=logits.device)
115	        num_rows = indices.shape[0]
116	    else:
117	        assert (
118	            logits_shape[0] == bitmask_shape[0]
119	        ), f"batch size mismatch: logits {logits_shape[0]} vs bitmask {bitmask_shape[0]}"
120	        num_rows = logits_shape[0]
121	
122	    if NUM_SMS > 0:
123	        grid = (NUM_SMS,)
124	    else:
125	        num_blocks = triton.cdiv(vocab_size, BLOCK_SIZE)
126	        grid = (num_rows * num_blocks,)
127	        NUM_SMS = triton.next_power_of_2(grid[0])
128	
129	    apply_token_bitmask_inplace_kernel[grid](
130	        logits,
131	        bitmask,
132	        indices,
133	        num_rows,
134	        vocab_size,
135	        logits_shape[1],
136	        bitmask_shape[1],
137	        NUM_SMS,
138	        BLOCK_SIZE,
139	        num_warps=BLOCK_SIZE // 32 // (16 // logits.element_size()),
140	        num_stages=3,
141	    )

650	async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput, request: Request):
651	    """Handle a generate request."""
652	    if obj.stream:
653	
654	        async def stream_results() -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
655	            try:
656	                async for out in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
657	                    obj, request
658	                ):
659	                    yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
660	                        out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
661	                    ) + b"\n\n"
662	            except ValueError as e:
663	                out = {"error": {"message": str(e)}}
664	                logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
665	                yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
666	                    out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
667	                ) + b"\n\n"
668	            yield b"data: [DONE]\n\n"
669	
670	        return StreamingResponse(
671	            stream_results(),
672	            media_type="text/event-stream",

152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(

28	)

40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None
99	
100	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
101	        return "", -1
102	
103	    def jump_and_retokenize(
104	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
105	    ):
106	        pass

108	
109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None

200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None

68	@dataclass
69	class RequestTimingMetricsMixin:
70	    """
71	    Mixin class containing common request-level timing metrics.
72	
73	    This class consolidates the timing metrics that are shared across all batch output types
74	    to avoid code duplication and ensure consistency.
75	    """
76	
77	    # Queue duration: time spent waiting in queue before request is scheduled.
78	    queue_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
79	
80	    # Forward entry time: timestamp when the request enters the forward pass stage.
81	    # This corresponds to `forward_entry_time` in TimeStats.
82	    # In different modes:
83	    #   - Unified/PD-colocate: timestamp when forward computation begins (covers prefill + decode)
84	    #   - Prefill instance (P): timestamp when prefill forward pass begins
85	    #   - Decode instance (D): timestamp when decode forward pass begins
86	    # Note: This is NOT the same as prefill_start_time. There may be a delay between
87	    # forward_entry_time and prefill_start_time (see prefill_launch_delay).
88	    forward_entry_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
89	
90	    # Prefill launch delay: time spent waiting between forward entry and prefill start.
91	    # Calculated as: prefill_start_time - forward_entry_time
92	    # This represents the delay between when the request enters the forward stage
93	    # and when prefill computation actually begins.
94	    prefill_launch_delay: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
95	
96	    # Prefill launch latency: time spent during prefill kernel launch.
97	    # Calculated as: prefill_end_time_host - prefill_start_time_host
98	    prefill_launch_latency: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
99	
100	    # Prefill finished time: timestamp when prefill phase completes (wall clock time).
101	    # This marks when the prefill computation finishes.
102	    prefill_finished_ts: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
103	
104	
105	@dataclass
106	class SpeculativeDecodingMetricsMixin:
107	    """
108	    Mixin class containing speculative decoding metrics.
109	
110	    This class consolidates speculative decoding metrics that are shared across
111	    batch output types that support speculative decoding to avoid code duplication.
112	    """
113	
114	    # Verify count: number of verification forward passes
115	    spec_verify_ct: List[int]
116	
117	    # Accepted tokens: Number of accepted tokens during speculative decoding
118	    spec_accepted_tokens: List[int]
119	
120	    # Acceptance histogram: List of lists, where each inner list represents histogram counts.
121	    # List index = number of accepted tokens in a step, List value = count of steps with that many accepted tokens.
122	    # Example: histogram[0] = 5 means 5 steps with 0 accepted tokens, histogram[3] = 10 means 10 steps with 3 accepted tokens.
123	    # Empty list [] when speculative decoding is disabled.
124	    spec_acceptance_histogram: List[List[int]]

260	    def _sample_from_logits(
261	        self,
262	        logits: torch.Tensor,
263	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
264	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
265	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
266	        """Sample from temperature-scaled logits without softmax.
267	
268	        Used for the Ascend NPU backend which handles softmax internally.
269	        """
270	        if simple_sampling_case:
271	            probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
272	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.multinomial(probs, num_samples=1).view(-1)
273	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
274	        else:
275	            assert (
276	                self.use_ascend_backend
277	            ), "Only ascend backend supports sampling from logits"
278	            batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_min_p_sampling_from_logits_ascend(
279	                logits,
280	                sampling_info.top_ks,
281	                sampling_info.top_ps,
282	                sampling_info.min_ps,
283	                sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling,
284	            )
285	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
286	
287	    def _forward_ascend_backend(
288	        self,
289	        logits: torch.Tensor,
290	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
291	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
292	        return_logprob: bool,
293	    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
294	        """Handle the full Ascend backend sampling path.
295	
296	        Ascend backend has fused kernels that handle softmax internally,
297	        so we sample directly from temperature-scaled logits.
298	
299	        Returns:
300	            A tuple of (batch_next_token_ids, logprobs). logprobs is None
301	            when return_logprob is False or SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB is set.
302	        """
303	        logits.div_(sampling_info.temperatures)
304	        batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_logits(
305	            logits, sampling_info, simple_sampling_case
306	        )
307	        logprobs = None
308	        if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
309	            logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
310	        return batch_next_token_ids, logprobs
311	
312	    def _attach_logprobs_to_output(
313	        self,
314	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
315	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
316	        top_logprobs_nums: List[int],
317	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
318	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
319	        batch_next_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
320	    ):
321	        # clamp to avoid -inf values
322	        logprobs.clamp_(min=torch.finfo(logprobs.dtype).min)
323	
324	        # Attach logprobs to logits_output (in-place modification)
325	        if any(x > 0 for x in top_logprobs_nums):
326	            (
327	                logits_output.next_token_top_logprobs_val,
328	                logits_output.next_token_top_logprobs_idx,
329	            ) = get_top_logprobs(logprobs, top_logprobs_nums)
330	
331	        if any(x is not None for x in token_ids_logprobs):
332	            (
333	                logits_output.next_token_token_ids_logprobs_val,
334	                logits_output.next_token_token_ids_logprobs_idx,
335	            ) = get_token_ids_logprobs(logprobs, token_ids_logprobs)
336	
337	        logits_output.next_token_logprobs = logprobs[
338	            torch.arange(len(batch_next_token_ids), device=sampling_info.device),
339	            batch_next_token_ids,
340	        ]

342	    def _sync_token_ids_across_tp(
343	        self, batch_next_token_ids: torch.Tensor, sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo
344	    ):
345	        if SYNC_TOKEN_IDS_ACROSS_TP or sampling_info.grammars:
346	            # For performance reasons, SGLang does not sync the final token IDs across TP ranks by default.
347	            # This saves one all-reduce, but the correctness of this approach depends on the determinism of several operators:
348	            # the last all-reduce, the last lm_head matmul, and all sampling kernels.
349	            # These kernels are deterministic in most cases, but there are some rare instances where they are not deterministic.
350	            # In such cases, enable this env variable to prevent hanging due to TP ranks becoming desynchronized.
351	            # When using xgrammar, this becomes more likely so we also do the sync when grammar is used.
352	
353	            torch.distributed.all_reduce(
354	                batch_next_token_ids,
355	                op=dist.ReduceOp.MIN,
356	                group=self.tp_sync_group,
357	            )

359	    def compute_logprobs_only(
360	        self,
361	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
362	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
363	        return_logprob: bool,
364	        top_logprobs_nums: List[int],
365	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
366	    ) -> None:
367	        """
368	        Compute logprobs for requested token IDs without performing sampling.
369	
370	        Optimized for prefill-only scoring requests that need token probabilities
371	        but don't require next token generation.
372	        """
373	
374	        if logits_output.next_token_logits is None:
375	            logger.warning("No logits available for logprob computation")
376	            return
377	
378	        # Check if any requests actually need logprobs computation
379	        needs_token_ids_logprobs = any(
380	            token_ids is not None and len(token_ids) > 0
381	            for token_ids in token_ids_logprobs
382	        )
383	        needs_top_logprobs = any(x > 0 for x in top_logprobs_nums)
384	
385	        if not (needs_token_ids_logprobs or needs_top_logprobs):
386	            return
387	
388	        # Preprocess logits (custom processors and NaN handling)
389	        logits = self._preprocess_logits(logits_output.next_token_logits, sampling_info)
390	
391	        # Compute logprobs
392	        logprobs = torch.nn.functional.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
393	
394	        # Handle top logprobs if requested
395	        if needs_top_logprobs:
396	            (
397	                logits_output.next_token_top_logprobs_val,
398	                logits_output.next_token_top_logprobs_idx,
399	            ) = get_top_logprobs(logprobs, top_logprobs_nums)
400	
401	        # Handle token_ids logprobs if requested
402	        if needs_token_ids_logprobs:
403	            (
404	                logits_output.next_token_token_ids_logprobs_val,
405	                logits_output.next_token_token_ids_logprobs_idx,
406	            ) = get_token_ids_logprobs_batch_optimized(logprobs, token_ids_logprobs)
