21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret

187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass

190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass

194	    def __len__(self):

55	    if not DISABLE_DISK_CACHE:

57	    else:
58	        return lambda fn: None

62	def init_state_to_jump_forward(regex_string):
63	    try:
64	        regex_pattern = interegular.parse_pattern(regex_string)
65	    except InvalidSyntax as e:
66	        logger.warning(f"skip invalid regex: {regex_string}, {e=}")
67	        return
68	
69	    byte_fsm = make_byte_level_fsm(regex_pattern.to_fsm().reduce(), keep_utf8=True)
70	    regex_fsm, _ = make_deterministic_fsm(byte_fsm)
71	
72	    fsm_info: FSMInfo = regex_fsm.fsm_info
73	
74	    symbol_to_id = fsm_info.alphabet_symbol_mapping
75	    id_to_symbol = {}
76	    for symbol, id_ in symbol_to_id.items():
77	        id_to_symbol.setdefault(id_, []).append(symbol)
78	
79	    transitions = fsm_info.transitions

    def _handle_grammar_backend(self):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            self.grammar_backend = "xgrammar"

    def _handle_mamba_backend(self):
        if self.mamba_backend == "flashinfer":

                try:
                    import flashinfer.mamba  # noqa: F401

                    logger.info("Successfully imported FlashInfer mamba module")

                    )

                raise ValueError(

                )

    def _handle_context_parallelism(self):
        if self.attn_cp_size > 1:
            # The tp_size is the world size, not the real tensor parallel size
            assert (
                self.tp_size % self.attn_cp_size == 0
            ), "tp_size must be divisible by attn_cp_size"
            assert (
                self.tp_size % (self.dp_size * self.attn_cp_size) == 0
            ), "tp_size must be divisible by dp_size * attn_cp_size"
            assert self.pp_size == 1, "PP is not supported with context parallelism"

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on how SGLang implements output constraints.

## Summary

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. **Constraint Definition and Storage**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 40-95)

The `SamplingParams` class defines constraint parameters:
```python
json_schema: Optional[str] = None        # JSON schema constraint
regex: Optional[str] = None              # Regex constraint  
ebnf: Optional[str] = None               # EBNF grammar constraint
structural_tag: Optional[str] = None     # Structural tag constraint
```

**Higher-level IR:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 36-40, 451-500)
- `SglSamplingParams` dataclass holds constraint definitions
- `SglGen` class wraps these parameters for generation operations

**API Layer:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)
- `gen()` function accepts `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
- `gen_int()`, `gen_string()` functions convert Python types to regex constraints via dtype conversion

### 2. **Constraint Registration and Backend Architecture**

**Base Classes:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

The factory pattern is implemented:
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}  # Registry for custom backends

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids) 
    -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    # Dispatches to registered backends or built-in implementations
```

Built-in backends:
- **`outlines`** - Uses Outlines library for constraint compilation
- **`xgrammar`** - Uses XGrammar library (highest performance)
- **`llguidance`** - Uses LLGuidance library
- **`none`** - Disables grammar-based constraints

### 3. **Constraint Type Dispatching**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 145-175)

The base class defines dispatch methods for each constraint type:
```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_pattern(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
```

### 4. **Backend Implementations**

**XGrammar Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` (lines 257-317)
```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles JSON schema via grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema()
        
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles regex via grammar_compiler.compile_regex()
        
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles EBNF via grammar_compiler.compile_grammar()
```

**Outlines Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
- Uses `build_regex_from_schema()` to convert JSON schemas to regex
- Implements `RegexGuide` for constraint application

**LLGuidance Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
- Uses `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()`
- Uses `grammar_from()` for regex and EBNF

### 5. **Base Constraint Object Interface**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 42-119)

All constraint implementations must inherit from `BaseGrammarObject`:
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:        # Accept a token
    def rollback(self, k: int):                         # Rollback k tokens
    def is_terminated(self):                            # Check if generation complete
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size, batch_size, device) -> torch.Tensor
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject"               # Create a copy for batching
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper) -> Tuple[str, int]
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state)
```

### 6. **Constraint Management in Generation Pipeline**

**Grammar Manager:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` (lines 67-105)

Handles constraint compilation and caching:
```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    # Determines constraint type and creates cache key
    if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
        key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
    elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
        key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
    elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
        key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
    elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
        key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
```

### 7. **Constraint Application During Sampling**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (lines 197-222)

Constraints are applied via vocabulary masking:
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    # Allocate mask tensor
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(...)
    
    # Fill mask with allowed tokens for each request
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    # Apply mask to logits
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

### 8. **Data Type to Constraint Conversion**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` (lines 127-157)

The dtype parameter is converted to regex constraints:
```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT        # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT      # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR        # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL       # r"(True|False)"
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

### 9. **Tool/Function Constraint Generation**

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on how SGLang implements output constraints.

## Summary

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. **Constraint Definition and Storage**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 40-95)

The `SamplingParams` class defines constraint parameters:
```python
json_schema: Optional[str] = None        # JSON schema constraint
regex: Optional[str] = None              # Regex constraint  
ebnf: Optional[str] = None               # EBNF grammar constraint
structural_tag: Optional[str] = None     # Structural tag constraint
```

**Higher-level IR:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 36-40, 451-500)
- `SglSamplingParams` dataclass holds constraint definitions
- `SglGen` class wraps these parameters for generation operations

**API Layer:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)
- `gen()` function accepts `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
- `gen_int()`, `gen_string()` functions convert Python types to regex constraints via dtype conversion

### 2. **Constraint Registration and Backend Architecture**

**Base Classes:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

The factory pattern is implemented:
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}  # Registry for custom backends

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids) 
    -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    # Dispatches to registered backends or built-in implementations
```

Built-in backends:
- **`outlines`** - Uses Outlines library for constraint compilation
- **`xgrammar`** - Uses XGrammar library (highest performance)
- **`llguidance`** - Uses LLGuidance library
- **`none`** - Disables grammar-based constraints

### 3. **Constraint Type Dispatching**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 145-175)

The base class defines dispatch methods for each constraint type:
```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_pattern(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
```

### 4. **Backend Implementations**

**XGrammar Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` (lines 257-317)
```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles JSON schema via grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema()
        
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles regex via grammar_compiler.compile_regex()
        
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles EBNF via grammar_compiler.compile_grammar()
```

**Outlines Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
- Uses `build_regex_from_schema()` to convert JSON schemas to regex
- Implements `RegexGuide` for constraint application

**LLGuidance Backend:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
- Uses `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()`
- Uses `grammar_from()` for regex and EBNF

### 5. **Base Constraint Object Interface**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 42-119)

All constraint implementations must inherit from `BaseGrammarObject`:
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:        # Accept a token
    def rollback(self, k: int):                         # Rollback k tokens
    def is_terminated(self):                            # Check if generation complete
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size, batch_size, device) -> torch.Tensor
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject"               # Create a copy for batching
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper) -> Tuple[str, int]
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state)
```

### 6. **Constraint Management in Generation Pipeline**

**Grammar Manager:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` (lines 67-105)

Handles constraint compilation and caching:
```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    # Determines constraint type and creates cache key
    if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
        key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
    elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
        key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
    elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
        key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
    elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
        key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
```

### 7. **Constraint Application During Sampling**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (lines 197-222)

Constraints are applied via vocabulary masking:
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    # Allocate mask tensor
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(...)
    
    # Fill mask with allowed tokens for each request
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    # Apply mask to logits
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

### 8. **Data Type to Constraint Conversion**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` (lines 127-157)

The dtype parameter is converted to regex constraints:
```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT        # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT      # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR        # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL       # r"(True|False)"
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

### 9. **Tool/Function Constraint Generation**

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` (lines 207-246)

For structured outputs (function calls):
```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    # Generates JSON schema constraint for tool schemas
    # Handles "required" choice (any tool), specific tool choice
    # Merges $defs from multiple tools
```

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Base classes and registry |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar backend (primary) |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Outlines backend |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance backend |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Constraint compilation & caching |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Constraint parameter definition |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` | Constraint mask application |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | IR-level constraint storage |
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | Public API for constraints |
| `python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` | Tool schema constraint generation |

### 11. **Constraint Lifecycle**

1. **Definition**: User specifies via `gen(regex=...)`, `gen(json_schema=...)`, or `dtype`
2. **Conversion**: dtype converted to regex in `_handle_dtype_to_regex()`
3. **Registration**: Constraint stored in `SamplingParams`
4. **Dispatch**: Grammar manager determines constraint type and dispatches to backend
5. **Compilation**: Backend compiles constraint to executable grammar (cached)
6. **Application**: Sampling layer applies vocabulary mask during token generation
7. **Validation**: Each generated token validated against constraint via `accept_token()`

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the comprehensive exploration report.

## Comprehensive SGLang Grammar Backend & Constrained Decoding Exploration

I've thoroughly explored SGLang's constrained decoding architecture. Here's a detailed breakdown:

---

## 1. ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

### Core File Structure
- **Base Classes**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`
- **Grammar Manager**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
- **Backend Implementations**:
  - XGrammar: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`
  - Outlines: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
  - LLGuidance: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
  - Reasoner Wrapper: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py`
- **Sampling Integration**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`
- **GPU Ops**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/triton_ops/bitmask_ops.py` and `/sgl-kernel/csrc/grammar/apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda.cu`

---

## 2. CLASS HIERARCHY

### Base Classes
```
BaseGrammarObject (abstract)
├── Properties & Methods:
│   ├── accept_token(token: int) → None
│   ├── rollback(k: int) → None
│   ├── is_terminated() → bool
│   ├── allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device) → torch.Tensor
│   ├── fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx) → None
│   ├── move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device) → torch.Tensor
│   ├── apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask) → None (static)
│   ├── copy() → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── try_jump_forward(tokenizer) → Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
│   ├── jump_forward_str_state(helper) → Tuple[str, int]
│   └── jump_and_retokenize(old_ids, new_ids, next_state) → None
└── GrammarStats dataclass:
    ├── compilation_time: float
    ├── schema_count: int
    ├── ebnf_size: int
    ├── is_cache_hit: bool
    ├── tree_traversal_time: List[float]
    ├── dispatch_type: str
    └── num_timeout: int

BaseGrammarBackend (abstract)
├── Properties:
│   ├── executor: ThreadPoolExecutor
│   └── cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry]
├── Dispatch Methods (override these for new constraint types):
│   ├── dispatch_json(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_regex(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_ebnf(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_structural_tag(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string) → BaseGrammarObject
│   └── _not_supported(key_type, key_string) → None
├── Core Methods:
│   ├── _init_value_dispatch(key, require_reasoning) → BaseGrammarObject
│   ├── get_cached_or_future_value(key, require_reasoning) → Tuple[Future|BaseGrammarObject, bool]
│   ├── set_cache(key, value) → None
│   └── reset() → None
└── Threading:
    └── Uses ThreadPoolExecutor for async grammar compilation
```

### Backend Implementations

**XGrammarGrammar** (uses xgrammar library)
- Wraps `xgrammar.GrammarMatcher`
- Uses bitmask-based token filtering (GPU-accelerated)
- Supports jump-forward optimization
- Methods: `accept_token()`, `rollback()`, `fill_vocab_mask()`, `apply_vocab_mask()`

**XGrammarGrammarBackend**
- Compiles constraints via `xgrammar.GrammarCompiler`
- `dispatch_json()`: Compiles JSON schema via `compile_json_schema()`
- `dispatch_regex()`: Compiles regex via `compile_regex()`
- `dispatch_ebnf()`: Compiles EBNF via `compile_grammar()`
- `dispatch_structural_tag()`: Compiles structural tags (JSON) via `compile_structural_tag()`

**OutlinesGrammar** (uses outlines library)
- Wraps `outlines.fsm.guide.RegexGuide`
- State-based matching (output as masked vocab)
- Jump-forward via `OutlinesJumpForwardMap`

**OutlinesGrammarBackend**
- `dispatch_json()`: Converts JSON schema to regex via `build_regex_from_schema()`, then compiles
- `dispatch_regex()`: Compiles regex directly

**GuidanceGrammar** (uses llguidance library)
- Wraps `llguidance.LLMatcher`
- Uses bitmask operations (GPU)
- Supports jump-forward tokens computation

**GuidanceBackend**
- `dispatch_json()`: Uses `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()`
- `dispatch_regex()`: Uses `grammar_from("regex", ...)`
- `dispatch_ebnf()`: Uses `grammar_from("ebnf", ...)`

**ReasonerGrammarBackend** (wrapper for extended thinking)
- Wraps another backend (`BaseGrammarBackend`)
- Postpones constraint enforcement until thinking ends
- Tracks `tokens_after_think_end` state

---

## 3. CONSTRAINT TYPES & COMPILATION FLOW

### Supported Constraint Types (in `SamplingParams`)

```python
class SamplingParams:
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None     # JSON Schema (mutually exclusive)
    regex: Optional[str] = None            # Regex pattern (mutually exclusive)
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None             # EBNF grammar (mutually exclusive)
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None   # Structural tags (can coexist)
```

### Key (Type, String) Pairs
The grammar system uses `Tuple[key_type: str, key_string: str]` as cache keys:
- `("json", json_schema_string)`
- `("regex", regex_pattern_string)`
- `("ebnf", ebnf_grammar_string)`
- `("structural_tag", json_serialized_tag)`
- `("structural_pattern", pattern_spec)` - Future extension
- `("structural_pattern_v2", pattern_spec)` - Future extension

### Compilation Pipeline

```
1. Request arrives with constraint → GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
2. Check cache: get_cached_or_future_value(key)
   - If cached: return copy, mark cache_hit
   - If not cached: submit async compilation task
3. Async dispatch (in thread pool):
   → _init_value_dispatch(key_type, key_string)
   → Select backend's dispatch_* method
   → Backend compiles constraint → BaseGrammarObject
4. Cache result: set_cache(key, value)
5. Integration:
   → GrammarManager polls for ready grammars
   → Attach to Req.grammar (Future or BaseGrammarObject)
6. Token generation:
   → SamplingBatchInfo allocates vocab_mask
   → Calls grammar.fill_vocab_mask() per request
   → Calls grammar.apply_vocab_mask(logits, mask)
   → Masked tokens get -inf logits
```

---

## 4. TOKEN MASKING & GPU INTEGRATION

### Vocab Mask Representation
- **Format**: Bitmask (1 bit per token = allowed, 0 = masked)
- **Shape**: `[batch_size, ceil(vocab_size / 32)]` (packed into int32)
- **Usage**: `fill_vocab_mask()` → `apply_vocab_mask()` → `-inf` logits

### GPU Acceleration
**Triton Kernel** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/triton_ops/bitmask_ops.py`):
- Function: `apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, bitmask, indices=None)`
- Sets masked tokens to `-inf` inplace on GPU
- 32-way word-level bitmask unpacking per thread block
- Configurable NUM_SMS (streaming multiprocessors) and BLOCK_SIZE

**CUDA Kernel** (`/sgl-kernel/csrc/grammar/apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda.cu`):
- HIP-compatible variant for AMD GPUs

### Sampling Integration
```python
# In SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask():
self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
    if grammar and not grammar.finished:
        grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, device)

# In apply_logits_bias():
if self.vocab_mask is not None:
    self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

---

## 5. GRAMMAR CACHING MECHANISM

### Cache Structure
```python
cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry]

@dataclass
class CacheEntry:
    value: BaseGrammarObject
    event: Event  # Threading synchronization
```

### Cache Hit Semantics
- **First compile**: Submitted to `ThreadPoolExecutor`, future stored in `req.grammar`
- **Subsequent requests**: Return `copy()` of cached grammar with stats reset
- **Compilation time**: `grammar_stats.is_cache_hit = True` on cache copies
- **Timeout handling**: If compilation takes >SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS, cached as `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ`

### Cache Invalidation
- `reset()`: Clears entire cache (typically per model reset)
- XGrammar backend: Also calls `GrammarCompiler.clear_cache()` for internal caching

---

## 6. ADDING NEW CONSTRAINT TYPES

### Step 1: Add to `SamplingParams`
```python
# In /python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py
class SamplingParams:
    my_custom_constraint: Optional[str] = None
    
    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
            self.my_custom_constraint,  # Add here
        ]
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one constraint type allowed")
```

### Step 2: Add Dispatch Type in `GrammarManager`
```python
# In /python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py, process_req_with_grammar():
elif req.sampling_params.my_custom_constraint is not None:
    key = ("my_custom", req.sampling_params.my_custom_constraint)
```

### Step 3: Implement Backend Methods

**Option A: Extend existing backend**
```python
# In XGrammarGrammarBackend:
def dispatch_my_custom(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        # Compile your constraint
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_my_custom(key_string)
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="my_custom"))
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid constraint: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
```

**Option B: Create custom backend**
```python
# Create /python/sglang/srt/constrained/my_backend.py
from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    BaseGrammarBackend, BaseGrammarObject, GrammarStats, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
)

class MyGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
    def __init__(self, constraint_engine, key_string):
        super().__init__()
        self.engine = constraint_engine
        self.grammar_stats = GrammarStats()
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        # Update engine state
        accepted = self.engine.accept(token)
        if not accepted:
            raise ValueError(f"Token {token} not accepted")
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size, batch_size, device):
        # Allocate and return bitmask tensor
        import torch
        return torch.ones((batch_size, (vocab_size + 31) // 32), dtype=torch.int32)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        # Set allowed tokens in vocab_mask[idx]
        allowed_tokens = self.engine.get_allowed_tokens()
        # Update vocab_mask[idx] with allowed token bits
    
    # ... implement other abstract methods

class MyGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, **kwargs):
        super().__init__()
        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
    
    def dispatch_my_custom(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[MyGrammarObject]:
        try:
            engine = compile_my_custom(key_string)
            obj = MyGrammarObject(engine, key_string)
            return obj
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Invalid constraint: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
```

### Step 4: Register Custom Backend (Optional)
```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import register_grammar_backend

def create_my_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids=None):
    return MyGrammarBackend(tokenizer)

register_grammar_backend("my_backend", create_my_backend)
```

### Step 5: Hook into Dispatch in `BaseGrammarBackend._init_value_dispatch()`
```python
# In base_grammar_backend.py:
elif key_type == "my_custom":
    grammar = self.dispatch_my_custom(key_string)
```

---

## 7. EXTENDING EXISTING BACKENDS

### XGrammar Backend Extensibility
XGrammar's `GrammarCompiler` is the core. To add support:
```python
# Must implement in xgrammar library first:
GrammarCompiler.compile_my_constraint(my_constraint_spec, ...)
```
Then expose via:
```python
def dispatch_my_constraint(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_my_constraint(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(...))
```

### Outlines Backend Extensibility
Requires regex conversion:
```python
def dispatch_my_constraint(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
    # Convert my_constraint to regex
    regex = convert_to_regex(key_string)
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

---

## 8. CONSTRAINT SPECIFICATION FORMATS

### JSON Schema Example
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
    "population": {"type": "integer"}
  },
  "required": ["name", "population"],
  "additionalProperties": false
}
```

### Regex Example
```
^user@example\.com$
```

### EBNF Example
```
rule = "hello" " " ("world" | "there")
```

### Structural Tags (Legacy)
```json
{
  "structures": [
    {"begin": "<tag>", "schema": {...}, "end": "</tag>"}
  ],
  "triggers": ["<tag>"]
}
```

### Structural Tags (New Format)
```json
{
  "format": {
    "type": "tag",
    "content": {
      "type": "json_schema",
      "json_schema": {...}
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 9. CONFIGURATION & REGISTRATION

### Server Args
```python
# /python/sglang/srt/server_args.py
grammar_backend: Optional[str] = None  # Defaults to "xgrammar"
constrained_json_whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
```

### Backend Selection in `create_grammar_backend()`
```python
if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:  # Custom backends first
    return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids)
elif name == "outlines":
    return OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "xgrammar":
    return XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "llguidance":
    return GuidanceBackend(...)
elif name == "none":
    return None
```

---

## 10. TEST PATTERNS

Test files show usage patterns:
- **JSON**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` - JSON schema validation
- **Regex**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` - Pattern matching
- **EBNF**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` - Grammar rules

---

## KEY INSIGHT: The Extensibility Model

The architecture uses **dispatch pattern** for constraint types:
1. **New constraint type** → Add `dispatch_my_type(key_string)` method
2. **New backend** → Implement all `dispatch_*` methods for supported types
3. **New compilation strategy** → Override dispatch method in custom backend
4. **Tokenizer-specific logic** → Use `tokenizer.init_xgrammar()` hook

The system is **fully pluggable**—you can add backends via `register_grammar_backend()` or extend existing ones by subclassing `BaseGrammarBackend`.

### Backend Implementations

### Supported Constraint Types (in `SamplingParams`)

```python
class SamplingParams:
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None     # JSON Schema (mutually exclusive)
    regex: Optional[str] = None            # Regex pattern (mutually exclusive)
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None             # EBNF grammar (mutually exclusive)
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None   # Structural tags (can coexist)
```

### Key (Type, String) Pairs
The grammar system uses `Tuple[key_type: str, key_string: str]` as cache keys:
- `("json", json_schema_string)`
- `("regex", regex_pattern_string)`
- `("ebnf", ebnf_grammar_string)`
- `("structural_tag", json_serialized_tag)`
- `("structural_pattern", pattern_spec)` - Future extension
- `("structural_pattern_v2", pattern_spec)` - Future extension

### Compilation Pipeline

```
1. Request arrives with constraint → GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
2. Check cache: get_cached_or_future_value(key)
   - If cached: return copy, mark cache_hit
   - If not cached: submit async compilation task
3. Async dispatch (in thread pool):
   → _init_value_dispatch(key_type, key_string)
   → Select backend's dispatch_* method
   → Backend compiles constraint → BaseGrammarObject
4. Cache result: set_cache(key, value)
5. Integration:
   → GrammarManager polls for ready grammars
   → Attach to Req.grammar (Future or BaseGrammarObject)
6. Token generation:
   → SamplingBatchInfo allocates vocab_mask
   → Calls grammar.fill_vocab_mask() per request
   → Calls grammar.apply_vocab_mask(logits, mask)
   → Masked tokens get -inf logits
```

---

## 4. TOKEN MASKING & GPU INTEGRATION

### Vocab Mask Representation
- **Format**: Bitmask (1 bit per token = allowed, 0 = masked)
- **Shape**: `[batch_size, ceil(vocab_size / 32)]` (packed into int32)
- **Usage**: `fill_vocab_mask()` → `apply_vocab_mask()` → `-inf` logits

### GPU Acceleration
**Triton Kernel** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/triton_ops/bitmask_ops.py`):
- Function: `apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, bitmask, indices=None)`
- Sets masked tokens to `-inf` inplace on GPU
- 32-way word-level bitmask unpacking per thread block
- Configurable NUM_SMS (streaming multiprocessors) and BLOCK_SIZE

**CUDA Kernel** (`/sgl-kernel/csrc/grammar/apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda.cu`):
- HIP-compatible variant for AMD GPUs

### Sampling Integration
```python
# In SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask():
self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
    if grammar and not grammar.finished:
        grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, device)

# In apply_logits_bias():
if self.vocab_mask is not None:
    self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

---

## 5. GRAMMAR CACHING MECHANISM

### Cache Structure
```python
cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry]

@dataclass
class CacheEntry:
    value: BaseGrammarObject
    event: Event  # Threading synchronization
```

### Cache Hit Semantics
- **First compile**: Submitted to `ThreadPoolExecutor`, future stored in `req.grammar`
- **Subsequent requests**: Return `copy()` of cached grammar with stats reset
- **Compilation time**: `grammar_stats.is_cache_hit = True` on cache copies
- **Timeout handling**: If compilation takes >SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS, cached as `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ`

### Cache Invalidation
- `reset()`: Clears entire cache (typically per model reset)
- XGrammar backend: Also calls `GrammarCompiler.clear_cache()` for internal caching

---

## 6. ADDING NEW CONSTRAINT TYPES

### Step 1: Add to `SamplingParams`
```python
# In /python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py
class SamplingParams:
    my_custom_constraint: Optional[str] = None
    
    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
            self.my_custom_constraint,  # Add here
        ]
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one constraint type allowed")
```

### Step 2: Add Dispatch Type in `GrammarManager`
```python
# In /python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py, process_req_with_grammar():
elif req.sampling_params.my_custom_constraint is not None:
    key = ("my_custom", req.sampling_params.my_custom_constraint)
```

### Step 3: Implement Backend Methods

**Option A: Extend existing backend**
```python
# In XGrammarGrammarBackend:
def dispatch_my_custom(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        # Compile your constraint
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_my_custom(key_string)
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="my_custom"))
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid constraint: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
```

**Option B: Create custom backend**
```python
# Create /python/sglang/srt/constrained/my_backend.py
from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    BaseGrammarBackend, BaseGrammarObject, GrammarStats, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
)

class MyGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
    def __init__(self, constraint_engine, key_string):
        super().__init__()
        self.engine = constraint_engine
        self.grammar_stats = GrammarStats()
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        # Update engine state
        accepted = self.engine.accept(token)
        if not accepted:
            raise ValueError(f"Token {token} not accepted")
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size, batch_size, device):
        # Allocate and return bitmask tensor
        import torch
        return torch.ones((batch_size, (vocab_size + 31) // 32), dtype=torch.int32)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        # Set allowed tokens in vocab_mask[idx]
        allowed_tokens = self.engine.get_allowed_tokens()
        # Update vocab_mask[idx] with allowed token bits
    
    # ... implement other abstract methods

class MyGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, **kwargs):
        super().__init__()
        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
    
    def dispatch_my_custom(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[MyGrammarObject]:
        try:
            engine = compile_my_custom(key_string)
            obj = MyGrammarObject(engine, key_string)
            return obj
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Invalid constraint: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
```

### Step 4: Register Custom Backend (Optional)
```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import register_grammar_backend

def create_my_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids=None):
    return MyGrammarBackend(tokenizer)

register_grammar_backend("my_backend", create_my_backend)
```

### Step 5: Hook into Dispatch in `BaseGrammarBackend._init_value_dispatch()`
```python
# In base_grammar_backend.py:
elif key_type == "my_custom":
    grammar = self.dispatch_my_custom(key_string)
```

---

## 7. EXTENDING EXISTING BACKENDS

### XGrammar Backend Extensibility
XGrammar's `GrammarCompiler` is the core. To add support:
```python
# Must implement in xgrammar library first:
GrammarCompiler.compile_my_constraint(my_constraint_spec, ...)
```
Then expose via:
```python
def dispatch_my_constraint(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_my_constraint(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(...))
```

### Outlines Backend Extensibility
Requires regex conversion:
```python
def dispatch_my_constraint(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
    # Convert my_constraint to regex
    regex = convert_to_regex(key_string)
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

---

## 8. CONSTRAINT SPECIFICATION FORMATS

### JSON Schema Example
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
    "population": {"type": "integer"}
  },
  "required": ["name", "population"],
  "additionalProperties": false
}
```

### Regex Example
```
^user@example\.com$
```

### EBNF Example
```
rule = "hello" " " ("world" | "there")
```

### Structural Tags (Legacy)
```json
{
  "structures": [
    {"begin": "<tag>", "schema": {...}, "end": "</tag>"}
  ],
  "triggers": ["<tag>"]
}
```

### Structural Tags (New Format)
```json
{
  "format": {
    "type": "tag",
    "content": {
      "type": "json_schema",
      "json_schema": {...}
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 9. CONFIGURATION & REGISTRATION

### Server Args
```python
# /python/sglang/srt/server_args.py
grammar_backend: Optional[str] = None  # Defaults to "xgrammar"
constrained_json_whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
```

### Backend Selection in `create_grammar_backend()`
```python
if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:  # Custom backends first
    return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids)
elif name == "outlines":
    return OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "xgrammar":
    return XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "llguidance":
    return GuidanceBackend(...)
elif name == "none":
    return None
```

---

## 10. TEST PATTERNS

Test files show usage patterns:
- **JSON**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` - JSON schema validation
- **Regex**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` - Pattern matching
- **EBNF**: `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` - Grammar rules

---

## KEY INSIGHT: The Extensibility Model

The architecture uses **dispatch pattern** for constraint types:
1. **New constraint type** → Add `dispatch_my_type(key_string)` method
2. **New backend** → Implement all `dispatch_*` methods for supported types
3. **New compilation strategy** → Override dispatch method in custom backend
4. **Tokenizer-specific logic** → Use `tokenizer.init_xgrammar()` hook

The system is **fully pluggable**—you can add backends via `register_grammar_backend()` or extend existing ones by subclassing `BaseGrammarBackend`.

13	# ==============================================================================
14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

## Summary of SGLang's Constraint Parameter Flow

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's how SGLang's `gen` function accepts and passes constraint parameters through the system:

### 1. **User-Facing API - The `gen()` Function**

**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (Lines 75-139)

**Function Signature**:
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
```

**Constraint Parameters Accepted**: `regex`, `json_schema`, `dtype`

The function validates regex before creating the IR object (line 113-115).

---

### 2. **Intermediate Representation Layer**

#### A. SglGen Class
**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (Lines 451-504)

The `gen()` function creates a `SglGen` IR node, passing all parameters including `dtype`, `regex`, and `json_schema` to the constructor, which then creates a `SglSamplingParams` object.

#### B. SglSamplingParams Dataclass
**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (Lines 18-139)

**Key Constraint Fields**:
- `dtype: Optional[str] = None` (Line 39) - for constrained generation, NOT included in to_xxx_kwargs
- `regex: Optional[str] = None` (Line 40) - for constrained generation, NOT included in to_xxx_kwargs
- `json_schema: Optional[str] = None` (Line 36) - included in to_srt_kwargs

**Backend-Specific Conversion Methods**:
- `to_openai_kwargs()` (Lines 64-77) - warns if regex is used
- `to_vertexai_kwargs()` (Lines 79-91) - warns if regex is used
- `to_anthropic_kwargs()` (Lines 93-107) - warns if regex is used
- `to_litellm_kwargs()` (Lines 109-119) - warns if regex is used
- `to_srt_kwargs()` (Lines 121-138) - includes `regex` and `json_schema`

---

### 3. **Interpreter/Execution Layer**

**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` (Line 569-621)

**Flow**:
1. `_execute_gen()` calls `_resolve_sampling_params()` on the SglGen's sampling_params
2. For runtime backends (not chat models with spec execution), calls `backend.generate(self, sampling_params=sampling_params)`

---

### 4. **Backend Layer - RuntimeEndpoint**

**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` (Lines 127-196)

**Critical Constraint Handling**:
- `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (Lines 127-157): Converts dtype to regex patterns
  - `int` → `REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"`
  - `float` → `REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"`
  - `str` → `REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""`
  - `bool` → `REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"`
  
- If both `dtype` and `regex` are set, dtype takes precedence (line 152-157)

**Request Creation** (Lines 159-196):
- Converts `SglSamplingParams` to dict via `sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()`
- Sends HTTP request to `/generate` endpoint with sampling_params dict

---

### 5. **HTTP Server Entry Point**

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/http_server.py` (Lines 650-683)

- Receives `GenerateReqInput` Pydantic model
- sampling_params field is `Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]]` (Line 191)

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/io_struct.py` (Lines 172-201)

`GenerateReqInput` dataclass accepts:
```python
sampling_params: Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]] = None
```

---

### 6. **Tokenizer Manager - Request Processing**

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` (Lines 490-527)

**Key Method**: `async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput)`

Flow:
1. Normalizes batch and arguments
2. Calls `_tokenize_one_request()` which calls `_create_tokenized_object()`

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` (Lines 894-964)

**`_create_tokenized_object()` Method**:
- Merges preferred_sampling_params with obj.sampling_params (dict)
- **Creates SamplingParams object** (Line 911):
  ```python
  sampling_params = self.sampling_params_class(**sampling_kwargs)
  ```
- Calls `sampling_params.normalize(self.tokenizer)` to normalize stop strings/regexes
- Calls `sampling_params.verify(self.model_config.vocab_size)` to validate constraints

---

### 7. **SamplingParams - SRT Backend**

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (Lines 31-163)

**Constructor Parameters** (Lines 40-67):
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    max_new_tokens: int = 128,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: float = 1.0,
    top_p: float = 1.0,
    top_k: int = -1,
    min_p: float = 0.0,
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
    min_new_tokens: int = 0,
    n: int = 1,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
    ignore_eos: bool = False,
    ...
):
```

**Constraint Validation** (Lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

---

### 8. **Request Object - Scheduler**

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py` (Lines 512-650)

**Req Class Constructor** receives `sampling_params: SamplingParams` (Line 520)

The `Req` object carries the fully resolved `SamplingParams` through the scheduler pipeline.

---

### 9. **Constraint Application - Sampling**

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (Lines 41-222)

**Grammar Fields**:
```python
grammars: Optional[List] = None
vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
```

**Constraint Initialization**:
- Grammar objects are created from `regex`, `json_schema`, or `ebnf`
- Used to fill vocab masks that filter logits during sampling

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` (Lines 114-191)

**OutlinesGrammarBackend Class** provides dispatch methods:
- `dispatch_json()` - converts json_schema to regex using `build_regex_from_schema()`
- `dispatch_regex()` - compiles regex to FSM guide
- Allocates vocab masks and applies them to logits

---

### 10. **Data Flow Summary**

```
User API (gen)
    ↓
SglGen IR node + SglSamplingParams
    ↓
Interpreter (_execute_gen)
    ↓
Backend.generate() with SglSamplingParams
    ↓
HTTP Request to /generate with sampling_params dict
    ↓
GenerateReqInput (Pydantic) receives dict
    ↓
TokenizerManager._create_tokenized_object()
    ↓
SamplingParams instantiation (**sampling_kwargs)
    ↓
sampling_params.normalize() + sampling_params.verify()
    ↓
TokenizedGenerateReqInput with SamplingParams object
    ↓
Req object in Scheduler with SamplingParams
    ↓
Grammar/Constraint Backend (Outlines)
    ↓
vocab_mask application during sampling
```

---

### Key File Paths:

1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/lang/api.py` - User-facing gen() function

3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` - Constraint pre-processing (dtype→regex)
4. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/srt/managers/io_struct.py` - GenerateReqInput
5. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` - Request processing and SamplingParams creation
6. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams class with constraint validation
7. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503766955-yzz0bw/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py` - Req class receives SamplingParams

Excellent! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the SGLang constraint system. Let me read the key files to get exact line numbers and understand the precise code structure before creating my plan.

The key insight is that a "json_path" constraint is fundamentally different from the existing grammar-based constraints (regex, json_schema, ebnf). Those constraints use grammar-guided decoding to restrict token generation at each step. A "json_path" constraint would need to:

1. Ensure the output is valid JSON (can reuse json_schema machinery)


2. Validate that specific JSON paths exist in the output

I'm considering three approaches: converting the json_path requirement into a JSON schema that enforces the path structure (cleanest option), post-validating generated JSON against the path (doesn't fit the constrained decoding paradigm well), or using json_schema decoding with an additional validation step. A path like `$.store.book[*].author` means the output must be valid JSON with a `store` object containing the required structure.

I can actually convert this json_path into a JSON schema that enforces the nested structure—requiring `store` at the root, `book` as an array within it, and `author` as a required field in each book element. This way I'm reusing the existing json_schema dispatch infrastructure...

Building the schema structure...

Now I need to look at the actual implementation files to understand how the constraint enforcement works during generation.

def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""

    if choices:
        return SglSelect(
            name,
            choices,
            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
        )

    # check regex is valid
    if regex is not None:
        try:
            re.compile(regex)
        except re.error as e:
            raise e

    return SglGen(
        name,
        max_tokens,
        min_tokens,
        n,
        stop,
        stop_token_ids,
        stop_regex,
        temperature,
        top_p,
        top_k,
        min_p,
        frequency_penalty,
        presence_penalty,
        ignore_eos,
        return_logprob,
        logprob_start_len,
        top_logprobs_num,
        return_text_in_logprobs,
        dtype,
        regex,
        json_schema,
    )


def gen_int(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,

class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    min_new_tokens: int = 0
    n: int = 1
    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
    min_p: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None

    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
    dtype: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None

    def clone(self):
        return SglSamplingParams(
            self.max_new_tokens,
            self.min_new_tokens,
            self.n,
            self.stop,
            self.stop_token_ids,
            self.stop_regex,
            self.temperature,
            self.top_p,
            self.top_k,
            self.min_p,
            self.frequency_penalty,
            self.presence_penalty,
            self.ignore_eos,
            self.return_logprob,
            self.logprob_start_len,
            self.top_logprobs_num,
            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
            self.json_schema,
        )

    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "n": self.n,
            "stop": self.stop or None,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
        }

    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn(
                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
            )
        return {
            "candidate_count": 1,
            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
        }

    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn(
                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
            )
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop_sequences": (
                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
            ),
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k,
        }

    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop": self.stop or None,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
        }

    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
        return {
            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
            "n": self.n,
            "stop": self.stop,
            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k,
            "min_p": self.min_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
            "regex": self.regex,
            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
        }


class SglFunction:
    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
        self.func = func
        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
        self.pin_prefix_rid = None

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
